column29: The patient was placed in a supine position with the neck extended using a shoulder roll. After induction of general anesthesia and endotracheal intubation, the neck and upper chest were prepped and draped in a sterile fashion.    A 6 cm low transverse cervical collar incision was made approximately 2 cm above the sternal notch. Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the sternal notch. Strap muscles were separated in the midline and retracted laterally.      Dissection was performed using a harmonic scalpel to minimize thermal spread and reduce blood loss. The middle thyroid veins were identified and divided. The superior thyroid vessels were divided close to the thyroid capsule to preserve the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve. The recurrent laryngeal nerves (RLNs) were identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.    The parathyroid glands were identified. The right superior parathyroids were preserved in situ with intact vascular pedicles. The left superior parathyroid gland was noted as cannot identify.      Hemostasis was confirmed. No significant bleeding noted. Redivac drain was placed and fixed with silk 2-0. The strap muscles and platysma were reapproximated with vicryl 3-0. Skin was closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl.    The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column29: - The patient was placed in supine position and underwent generalized anesthesia  - The skin of right chest and axillar with right thigh were cleaned and draped with antiseptic solution (using separate set).   - The incision was marked incorporating th e NAC and all grossly involved skin including the fungating ulcer. The incision was made with scalpel and carried through subcutaneous tissue using electrocautery, Skin flaps were raised in a limited but adequate fashion to achieve gross clearance of involved skin while preserving viable flap perfusion  - Dissection proceeded to the chest wall. The breast tissue was removed from medial to lateral off the pectoralis major. Any adherent tissue was excised en bloc with the specimen.  - Hemostasis was achieved with electrocautery and ligation with silk as needed.   - Visible lymphatic channel was ligated using silk 3-0  - The wound was irrigated with H2O1, providine and NSS   - Hemostasis was achieved  - The edge of the wound was approximate to shrink down the size of the wound using atraumatic silk 2-0  - Then the attention was turned to the right thigh (donor site). Using a dermatome set to 0.014 inch (thickness), a split thickness skin graft measuring approximately 3 inches in width 6 inches in length x 2 (harvest from anteriomedial and anteriolateral thigh) were harvest.   - The graft was then meshed 1:3 and trimmed to fit the defect  - It was laid over the chest wall defect without tension and secured with absorable sutures. A vaccuum dressing was performed at recipient site  - Donor site: hemostasis was obtained with epinephrine-soaked gauze and dressed with bactrigas

column29: The patient was placed in supine position and underwent generalized anesthesia. The skin of right breast was cleaned and draped with antiseptic solution.     A curvilinear incision was made peri-areolar region 3 cm over the marking area (12 o'clock). Dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue using electrocautery. The breast tissue was dissected circumferentially.    The lesion was excised en bloc along with surrounding breast tissue, ensuring adequate margin by intra operative U/S confirmation.    The specimen was excised and oriented with sutures for margin identification (short strand - superior, long strand - lateral, double strands - anterior).     Bleeding was checked and hemostasis was achieved with electrocautery.     Specimen was confirmed margin by using intra-operative U/S, seen mass within specimen and free all margins.    The breast tissue was re-approximated using vicryl 3-0.    Skin edges were approximated with Vicryl 3-0 deep dermal sutures, and the skin was closed with Monocryl 5-0 subcuticular sutures.     The wound was cleansed, dried, and dressed with sterile gauze.
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column28: - Normal thyroid parenchyma   - Normal vascular gland   - RLNs, External branch of superior laryngeal nerve identified and preserved bilaterally  - Cannot dentify Lt inferior parathyroid gland  - No lymphadenopathy or invasion no adhesion bilobe  - Normal bilateral vocal cord movement after procedure
column29: - The patient was placed in a supine position with the neck extended using a shoulder roll - - After induction of general anesthesia and endotracheal intubation, the neck and upper chest were prepped and draped in a sterile fashion  - A 6 cm low transverse cervical collar incision was made approximately 1 cm above the sternal notch.   - Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the sternal notch. Strap muscles were separated in the midline and retracted laterally.  - Dissection was performed using electrocalterization and harmonic scalpel to minimize thermal spread and reduce blood loss.   - The middle thyroid veins were identified and divided. The superior thyroid vessels were divided close to the thyroid capsule to preserve the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve.   - The recurrent laryngeal nerves (RLNs), external branch of superior laryngeal nerve were identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.  - The parathyroid glands were identified. The right superior, inferior and left superior parathyroids were preserved in situ with intact vascular pedicles.   - The left inferior was noted as cannot identify.  - Hemostasis was confirmed. No significant bleeding noted.   - Redivac drain was placed and fixed with silk 2-0.   - The strap muscles and platysma were reapproximated with chromic catgutl 3-0.   - Skin was closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl.  - The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column29: -The patient was in supine position and general anesthesia was induced.  -Patent V blue dye and ICG were injected surrounding the subareolar region  -The breast, chest wall, axilla, and upper arm and neck were prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.  -A incision was made that encompassed the breast mass, the nipple-areola complex and the previous biopsy scar and passed in a generally transverse direction across the breast   -The lateral side of incision adjacent with axilla was dissected  -A hand-held ICG probe was used to identify the location of the LN spot in the axilla.  -Blue and green nodes were identified and the nodes were excised in its entirety.  -The bed of the node re-examined with ICG probe and no additional node was detected  -Flaps were raised in the avascular plane between subcutaneous tissue and breast tissue from the clavicle superiorly, the sternum medially, the anterior rectus sheath inferiorly, and past the lateral border of the pectoralis major muscle laterally.   -Hemostasis was achieved in the flaps.   -The breast tissue and underlying pectoralis fascia were excised from the pectoralis major muscle, progressing from medially to laterally.   -At the lateral border of the pectoralis major muscle, the breast tissue was swung laterally and a lateral pedicle identified where breast tissue gave way to fat of axilla.   -The lateral pedicle was incised, ligated and the specimen removed.  -The wound was irrigated and hemostasis was achieved.   -A radivac suction drain was/ closed suction drains were brought into the operative field through a separate stab incision.  -The wound was closed with interrupted 3-0 Vicryl to the subcutaneous layer, followed by a subcuticular layer of 4-0 Vicryl.

column29: - General anesthesia was induced, and prophylactic antibiotics were administered. A peri-areolar injection of patent V blue was performed. A closed-suction drain was placed in the mastectomy bed. The wound was closed in layers using absorbable sutures with vicryl 4,0 subcutaneously interrupted fashion and skin was closed using vicryl 4,0 in continuous running fashion . column28: Right breast  -non palpable Rt breast lesion was marked by Radiologist at R9  -Entire target lesion had been completed resected with radiology confirmation  -No additional masses were palpate    Left breast   -No clinically abnormal Lt breast mass or axillary LN was palpated  -Normal Lt breast skin  -No nipple retraction  -SLN negative 0/3
column29: -The patient was placed supine and after GA bilateral chest and axilla were prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.    Rt breast   -Preoperative US localization non palpable Rt breast mass at R9 was performed by radiology.  A transverse incision was made overlying the marking that was planned in such a way as to minimize the amount of dissection to reach the mass.  -Flaps were raised and the dissection was then taken down circumferentially, taking care to include the entire localizing marking and a wide margin of grossly normal tissue.  -The specimen was removed and oriented for radiologic ultrasound confirmation  -Radiologist confirm that the entire target lesion had been completed resected.   -The wound was irrigated  -Hemostasis was checked  -The wound was closed with interrupted sutures of 4-0 Vicryl and a subcuticular suture of 5-0 Monocryl    Lt breast  -Patent V blue dye and ICG were injected surrounding Lt. subareolar region  -An incision was made that encompassed L2 breast mass and the nipple-areola complex passing in elliptical oblique direction across the breast   -A separated Lt lowest axillary hairline was made  -A hand-held ICG probe was used to identify the location of the LN spot in the Lt. axilla.  -A blue and green nodes were identified and the nodes were excised in its entirety.  -The bed of the node re-examined with ICG probe and no additional node was detected  -Flaps were raised in the avascular plane between subcutaneous tissue and breast tissue from the clavicle superiorly, the sternum medially, the anterior rectus sheath inferiorly, and past the lateral border of the pectoralis major muscle laterally.   -Hemostasis was achieved in the flaps.   -The breast tissue and underlying pectoralis fascia were excised from the pectoralis major muscle, progressing from medially to laterally.   -At the lateral border of the pectoralis major muscle, the breast tissue was swung laterally and a lateral pedicle identified where breast tissue gave way to fat of axilla. The lateral pedicle was incised, ligated and the specimen removed.  -The wound was irrigated and hemostasis was achieved.     Reconstruction phase   -Plastic surgery team was arrived in operative field and Silicone reconstruction was performed at Lt chest  -A radivac suction drain was brought into the operative field through a separate stab incision.  -The wound was closed by plastic surgery team

column28: -Incarcerated Rt IIH, Funicular type  -Omental content in hernia sac  -Good omental viability  -Minimal clear yellow ascites
column29: -The patient was in supine and  general anesthesia was induced.   -The right groin was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.   -A transverse incision was made in Rt. lowest skin crease and deepened through Scarpa's and Camper's fascia with electrocautery until the aponeurosis of the external oblique was encountered.   -This was cleaned and the external ring was exposed.   -An incision was made in the midportion of the external oblique aponeurosis in the direction of its fibers.   -The ilioinguinal nerve was identified and protected throughout the dissection.   -Flaps of the external oblique were developed cephalad and inferiorly.  -The cord with large hernia sac were identified.   -It was gently dissected free at the pubic tubercle and encircled with a Penrose drain.   -An indirect hernia sac was identified at the anteromedial aspect of the cord   -The sac was carefully dissected free of the cord down to the level of the internal ring.   -The vas deferens and testicular vessels were identified and protected.  -The sac was opened and omental contents were reduced.   -The sac was twisted and suture ligated with 2-0 silk.   -The stump of the sac was checked for hemostasis and allowed to retract into the abdomen.  -Attention was then turned to the floor of the canal, the Ultra pro mesh was cut to the appropriate size and trimmed as needed.   -Beginning at the pubic tubercle, the mesh was sutured to the inguinal ligament inferiorly and the conjoint tendon superiorly using two continuous running 2-0 Prolene sutures.  -Laterally, the tails of the mesh were crossed and the internal ring was recreated  -Hemostasis was checked.  -The Penrose drain was removed.   -The external oblique aponeurosis was closed with a running suture of 2-0 Vicryl,   -Scarpa's fascia was closed with interrupted 2-0 Vicryl.   -The skin was closed with Nylon 3-0

column28: - Subcentimeter thyroid nodule at left upper pole, yellowish, hard consistency  - Preserved left superior and inferior parathyroid glands  - Preserved left recurrent laryngeal nerve in tracheoesophageal groove, posterior to inferior thyroid artery  - Preserved internal branch of left superior laryngeal nerve  - Not identify external branch of left superior laryngeal nerve   - Thyroglossal duct cyst inferior to hyoid bone level   - Normal trachea
column29: - Patient was in supine position with neck hyperextension  - Skin was prepped and draped in sterile technique.  - Collar incision was made at 3 FB above sternal notch.  - Subplatysmal flap was created.  - Anterior jugular vein was ligated with Silk 3/0 and Harmonic.  - Upper flap was mobilized cranially up to thyroid cartilage. Lower flap was mobilized caudally down to sternal notch.  - Strap muscle (Sternohyoid and Sternothyroid) was separated in the middle and retracted laterally.  - Pre-tracheal fascia was incised in midline.    - Middle thyroid vein was ligated with Harmonic.  - Left superior pole of thyroid was dissected from underlying tissue, using Harmonic.  - Left thyroid nodule was identified.  - Left superior thyroid artery was identified and ligated proximally to its branches. Internal branch of superior laryngeal nerve was preserved. External branch of superior laryngeal nerve was not identified.  - Left upper and lower parathyroid glands were identified and preserved.  - Left recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and preserved in tracheoesophageal groove, posterior to inferior thyroid artery.  - Left lobe of thyroid was dissected from Berry's ligament. Pyramidal lobe was identified and dissected.    - Thyroglossal duct was identified and followed. Thyroglossal duct cyst was found inferior to hyoid bone with connecting duct superior to hyoid bone.  - Thyroglossal duct cyst was accidentally ruptured during dissection. The content was suctioned.  - Bone cutter was used to cut hyoid bone out.  - Thyroglossal duct was tapering at base of tongue level, ligated with Silk 2/0 and disconnected.    - Wound was cleaned with Hydrogen peroxide, Povidine and NSS.  - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Bone wax was used to stop bleeding at hyoiod bone.  - Surgicel was placed at the area of excised thyroglossal duct cyst and left thyroid.  - Subplatysmal flap was closed with Vicryl 3/0, continuous suture.  - Skin was closed with Monocryl 4/0, running subcuticular stitch.

column29: - The patient was placed in a supine position with neck extension   - After induction of general anesthesia and endotracheal intubation, the neck and upper chest were prepped and draped in a sterile fashion  - A 6 cm low transverse cervical collar incision was made approximately 1FB above the sternal notch.   - Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the sternal notch. Strap muscles were separated in the midline and retracted laterally.  - Dissection was performed on Rt lobe first using electrocauterization and harmonic scalpel to minimize thermal spread and reduce blood loss.   - The middle thyroid veins were identified and divided. The superior thyroid vessels were divided close to the thyroid capsule to preserve the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve.   - The recurrent laryngeal nerves (RLNs) was identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.  - Then Lt thyroid gland was dissected from surrounding muscle and trachea   - Hemostasis was confirmed. No significant bleeding noted.   - Redivac drain was placed and fixed with silk 3-0.   - The strap muscles and platysma were reapproximated with vicryl 3-0  - Skin was closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl.  - The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

the usual sterile fashion.  -Indigo carmine was injected surrounding lesional marking    -The lumpectomy was performed first.   -A transverse incision was made overlying the localizing wire.  -Flaps were developed and the wire was delivered into the wound  -Dissection was then taken down circumferentially, taking care to include the entire localizing needle and a wide margin of grossly normal tissue  -The specimen and entire localizing wire were removed and to radiology studies.  -Confirmation was received that the entire target lesion had been resected.  -There was close margin to anterior and posterior mass surface   -Pectolaris muscle and anterior surface of Rt breast mass were wedge resected  -No additional masses were palpated.    -A horizontal skin incision was made in a skin crease just below the hair-bearing skin of the axilla  -Flaps were raised cephalad and caudad to the estimated level of the axillary vein superiorly and the edge of the pectoralis major medially  -The clavipectoral fascia was then incised along the edge of the pectoralis major and the pectoralis major and minor were freed from surrounding fat and nodal tissue.   -Dissection progressed first under the pectoralis major and then under the pectoralis minor muscle.  -The pectoral muscles were retracted medially.   -The medial pectoral neurovascular bundle was identified and preserved.   -The level II nodal tissue deep to the pectoralis minor was included in the dissection.   -The axillary vein was then identified and cleared of overlying fat.   -The thoracodorsal nerve was then identified and preserved.   -The long thoracic nerve was then identified along on the chest wall and preserved.    -All wounds were irrigated and hemostasis was achieved.   -A closed-suction drain was brought into the axillary operative field through a separate stab incision  -All wound were closed with interrupted 3-0 Vicryl to the subcutaneous layer, followed by a subcuticular layer of 4-0 Monocryl

column29: -A 8-cm incision was made in a skin crease positioned approximately two finger breadths superior to the sternal notch.   -The subcutaneous tissues and platysma were divided   -Subplatysmal flaps were then raised inferiorly extending to the sternal notch and superiorly extending to the thyroid cartilage.   -The strap muscles were divided in the midline and retracted laterally.  -The right thyroid lobe was mobilized from its areolar attachments using blunt dissection, electrocautery and Hamonic energy device.   -The right thyroid gland was then gently retracted inferomedially.   -The superior pole vessels were ligated with 2-0 silk and divided close to the thyroid gland, with careful attention not to injure the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve.  -The thyroid gland was gently retracted medially and the muscles retracted laterally using blunt dissection  -The Rt. inferior thyroid vessels were ligated with 3-0 silk ties and divided with great care to avoid injury to the recurrent laryngeal nerve and to preserve    -The  Rt. superior and inferior parathyroid glands were identified and carefully preserved  -The thyroid was dissected medially from its attachments to the trachea up to the ligament of Berry  -Dissection was extended to the isthmus.  -A similar procedure was then carried out on the left thyroid gland   -The left recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and carefully followed superficially along its course superiorly to ensure it was not injured.   -The left superior and inferior parathyroid glands was identified but only left inferior parathyroid gland was preserved   -Dissection was extended to the isthmus, dissecting the thyroid free from the trachea.   -The specimen was removed  -The hemostasis was achieved  -The strap muscles deep cervical fascia were reapproximated with a running 3-0 Vicryl sutures  -The skin was closed with a running 4-0 Vicryl subcuticular suture.

column29: - The patient was placed in supine position and underwent generalized anesthesia   - Then the patient was placed in neck extension and heads up  - A collar incision was made approximately 2 FBs above the sternal notch  - Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the sternal notch.  - The strap muscles were divided in the midline and retracted laterally to expose the thyroid glands  - The right thyroid lobe was mobilized first. - The right middle thyroid vein was identified, ligated and divided using harmonic scalpel.  - The right recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified in the tracheo-esophageal groove and carefully preserved.   - The right superior pole vessels were individually ligated and divided close to the thyroid capsule, preserving the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve using harmonic scalpel.  - The right superior and inferior parathyroid glands were identified and preserved with their vascular supply intact.   - The right lobe was dissected off the trachea and the isthmus was divided and removed along with the right thyroid lobe  - Attention was then turned to the left side, The left thyroid lobe was mobilized in a similar fashion  - The left middle thyroid vein was ligated and divided. The left superior pole vessels were ligated close to the thyroid capsule  - The left recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and preserved. The left superior and inferior parathyroid glands were identified and preserved. The left thyroid lobe was dissected free from trachea and removed, completing the total thyroidectomy  - Hemostasis was meticulously achieved. Surgicel was placed at both side of trachea..  - Strap muscle was closed using Catgut 3-0 in continuous fashion  - Platysma was closed using vicryl 4-0 in interrupted suture  - Skin was closed using vicryl 4-0 in subcuticular suture

column29: - Patient was in supine position with slightly hyperextended neck, under general anesthesia.  - Neck and left forearm was prepped and draped in sterile technique.  - Low collar incision was done.  - Subplatysmal flaps were created. Upper flap was mobilized cranially up to thyroid cartilage. Lower flap was mobilized caudally up to suprasternal notch.  - Left anterior jugular vein was ligated with Harmonic. Right anterior jugular vein was preserved.  - Strap muscles were separated in the middle and retracted laterally.  - Pre-tracheal fascia was opened in midline.  - Dissection started from right side. Lateral part of right thyroid lobe was dissected and mobilized medially. Right inferior parathyroid gland was identified and removed using harmonic and peanuts.  - Right superior parathyroid gland was identified and removed.  -  Left lobe of thyroid was mobilized medially. Left inferior parathyroid gland was identified and removed.  - Superior parathyroid gland was identified and removed.  - Wound was cleaned with NSS.  - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Strap muscle was approximated with Vicyrl 3-0, continuous suture.  - Platysma muscle was closed with Vicryl 4-0.  - Skin was closed with subcuticular stitch, Monocryl 4-0.    Autotransplatation :  - Half of left superior parathyroid gland was cut into pieces.  - Small linear incision was done at left forearm.  - Pocket at brachioradialis muscle was created.  - Parathyroid graft fragments were reimplanted and sutured with silk  - Skin was closed with subcuticular stitch, Monocryl 4-0.

Upper and Lt upper parathyroid glands cannot be identified
column29: -A  6-cm incision was made in a skin crease positioned approximately one finger breadths superior to the sternal notch.   -The subcutaneous tissues and platysma were divided   -Subplatysmal flaps were then raised inferiorly extending to the sternal notch and superiorly extending to the thyroid cartilage.   -The strap muscles were divided in the midline and retracted laterally.  -The left thyroid lobe was mobilized from its areolar attachments using blunt dissection, electrocautery and Hamonic energy device.   -The thyroid was gently retracted medially and the middle thyroid vein was identified and ligated with 3-0 silk   -Lt superior lobe nodule was attached with strap muscle that the muscle was wedge resected  -The left thyroid gland was then gently retracted inferomedially. The superior pole vessels were ligated with 2-0 silk sutures and divided close to the thyroid gland, with careful attention not to injure the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve.  -The thyroid gland was gently retracted medially and the muscles retracted laterally using blunt dissection  -The Lt. inferior thyroid vessels were ligated with 3-0 silk ties and divided with great care to avoid injury to the recurrent laryngeal nerve and to preserve their distal branches.   -The  Lt. superior parathyroid glands were identified and carefully preserved but Lt inferior parathyroid gland was excision including Lt. lower pole thyroid gland  -The thyroid was dissected medially from its attachments to the trachea up to the ligament of Berry  -Dissection was extended to the isthmus.  -The pyramidal lobe was identified. It was dissected free from its attachments over the thyroid cartilage and then reflected inferiorly down to the isthmus.  -A similar procedure was then carried out on the right.   -The right recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and carefully followed superficially along its course superiorly to ensure it was not injured.   -The right inferior parathyroid glands was identified and preserved.   -Dissection was extended to the isthmus, dissecting the thyroid free from the trachea.   -The specimen was removed  -The wound was copiously irrigated and hemostasis was achieved  -Radivac drain was placed  -The strap muscles and platysma were reapproximated with interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures.  -Pre-tracheal was reapproximated with a running 3-0 Vicryl sutures  -The skin was closed with a running 4-0 Monocryl subcuticular suture.

column29: -The patient was brought to the operating room and the side of surgery was verified.   -General anesthesia was induced.   -The right chest and axilla were then prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.  -A transverse elliptical incision was made overlying the palpable mass at lateral chest wall  -Flaps were developed and the palpable mass was resected taken down circumferentially, taking care to include a wide margin of grossly normal tissue.    -The dissection was continue lateral, cephalad and caudad to the estimated level of the axillary vein superiorly and the edge of the pectoralis major medially.   -The clavipectoral fascia was then incised along the edge of the pectoralis major and the pectoralis major and minor were freed from surrounding fat and nodal tissue.   -Dissection progressed first under the pectoralis major and then under the pectoralis minor muscle.  -The pectoral muscles were retracted medially  -The medial pectoral neurovascular bundle was identified and preserved.   -The level II nodal tissue deep to the pectoralis minor was included in the dissection.   -The axillary vein was then identified and cleared of overlying fat.   -The thoracodorsal nerve was then identified and preserved.   -The long thoracic nerve was then identified along the edge of the latissimus dorsi on the chest wall and preserved.   -The remaining nodal tissue between these nerves was then carefully removed, taking care to protect the nerves.   The specimen including Rt chest wall mass and axillary content were removed  -The wound was irrigated and hemostasis was achieved.   -A radivac drain was brought into the operative field through a separate stab incision and sutured to the skin with a 3-0 silk suture.   -The wound was closed with interrupted 4-0 Vicryl to the subcutaneous layer, followed by a subcuticular layer of 4-0 Vicryl.

(Frozen section could not be performed due to frozen-section machine malfunction)  - No grossly enlarged or suspicious axillary lymph nodes apart from sentinel nodes.  - Hemostasis was secured with no active bleeding.  - Surgical margins appeared grossly free of tumor.
column29: - General anesthesia was induced, and prophylactic antibiotics were administered. A peri-areolar injection of patent V blue was performed. The left breast and axilla were prepared and draped in a sterile fashion.  - An elliptical incision was made incorporating the nipple-areolar complex.  dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue. Using visual identification.  - the sentinel lymph nodes were identified and excised. The lymphatic channels were ligated. Hemostasis was achieved.  - Skin flaps were raised superiorly to the clavicle, medially to the sternum, inferiorly to the inframammary fold, and laterally to the latissimus dorsi. The breast tissue was dissected off the pectoralis major fascia and removed en bloc.  - The operative field was inspected, and meticulous hemostasis was achieved. A closed-suction drain was placed in the mastectomy bed. The wound was closed in layers using absorbable sutures. Sterile dressing was applied.

column27: Low collar incision (2 FB above sternal notch)
column28: - Four normal parathyroid glands  - Suspected thyroid nodule at mid part of right thyroid lobe, near right superior parathyroid gland
column29: - Patient was in supine position with slightly hyperextended neck, under general anesthesia.  - Neck and left forearm was prepped and draped in sterile technique.  - Low collar incision was done.  - Subplatysmal flaps were created. Upper flap was mobilized cranially up to thyroid cartilage. Lower flap was mobilized caudally up to suprasternal notch.  - Left anterior jugular vein was ligated with Harmonic. Right anterior jugular vein was preserved.  - Strap muscles were separated in the middle and retracted laterally.  - Pre-tracheal fascia was opened in midline.  - Dissection started from left side. Lateral part of left thyroid lobe was dissected and mobilized medially. Left inferior parathyroid gland was identified and removed using harmonic and peanuts.  - Left superior parathyroid gland was identified and removed.  -  Right lobe of thyroid was mobilized medially. Right inferior parathyroid gland was identified and removed.  - Superior parathyroid gland and nearby suspected thyroid nodule were identified and removed.  - Wound was cleaned with NSS.  - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Radivac drain was placed xI.  - Strap muscle was approximated with Chromic catgut 3-0, continuous suture.  - Platysma muscle was closed with Vicryl 4-0.  - Skin was closed with subcuticular stitch, Monocryl 4-0.    Autotransplatation :  - Half of left superior parathyroid gland was cut into pieces.  - Small linear incision was done at left forearm.  - Pocket at brachioradialis muscle was created.  - Parathyroid graft fragments were reimplanted and sutured with silk  - Skin was closed with subcuticular stitch, Monocryl 4-0.
column30: - Four parathyroid glands were sent for frozen section. Confirmation of four parathyroid glands were reported.  - One right thyroid nodule
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column29: - The patient was placed in supine position with the neck extended using a shoulder roll.  - After induction of general anesthesia and endotracheal intubation, the neck and upper chest were prepped and draped in sterile technique.  - A low transverse collar incision (about 4 cm) was made two fingerbreadths above the sternal notch.  - Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the suprasternal notch  - Strap muscle was separated in the midline and retraced laterally  - The left thyroid lobe was mobilized and dissected using Harmonic scalpel to minimized thermal spread and reduce blood loss  - The middle thyroid vein were identified and ligated with atraumatic silk 3-0  - Superior thyroid artery and vein were individual ligated with Silk to preserve external branch of superior laryngeal nerve  - The Left recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.  - Careful identified and harmonic dissection was performed to retrieve thyroid gland.  - Inferior thyroid artery and vein were individual ligated with atraumatic silk 2-0  - Left, right thyroid gland and isthmus was divided and removed   - Hemostasis and bleeding was checked and stopped with electrocautery device.   - Surgicel x 2 was applied on raw surface  - Radivac drain No 8 was placed  - Strap muscles and plastysma were reapproximated with 4-0 Vicryl  - Subcutaneous tissue closed with 4-0 Vicryl  - Subdermal layer approximated with 4-0 Vicryl and skin closed with 4-0 Monocryl, subcuticular fashion.

column28: - The right breast was removed en bloc with overlying skin and nipple-areolar complex.  - No gross invasion of tumor to the pectoralis major muscle.  - No obvious chest wall involvement.  - Sentinel lymph node were identified using patent V blue and were successfully identified and excised -> frozen section cannot be sent due to machine dysfunction  - No grossly enlarged or suspicious axillary lymph nodes apart from sentinel nodes.  - Hemostasis was secured with no active bleeding.  - Surgical margins appeared grossly free of tumor.
column29: - General anesthesia was induced, and prophylactic antibiotics were administered. A peri-areolar injection of patent V blue was performed. The right breast and axilla were prepared and draped in a sterile fashion.  - An elliptical incision was made incorporating the nipple-areolar complex.  dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue. Using visual identification.  - the sentinel lymph nodes were identified and excised. The lymphatic channels were ligated. Hemostasis was achieved.  - Skin flaps were raised superiorly to the clavicle, medially to the sternum, inferiorly to the inframammary fold, and laterally to the latissimus dorsi. The breast tissue was dissected off the pectoralis major fascia and removed en bloc.  - The operative field was inspected, and meticulous hemostasis was achieved. A closed-suction drain was placed in the mastectomy bed. The wound was closed in layers using absorbable sutures. Sterile dressing was applied.

column29: The patient was placed in supine position and underwent generalized anesthesia. The skin of right breast was cleaned and draped with antiseptic solution.     A curvilinear incision was made 4 cm over the marking area (12 o'clock). Dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue using electrocautery. The breast tissue was dissected circumferentially.    The lesion was excised en bloc along with surrounding breast tissue, ensuring adequate margin by intra operative U/S confirmation.    The specimen was excised and oriented with sutures for margin identification (short strand - superior, long strand - lateral, double strands - anterior).     Bleeding was checked and hemostasis was achieved with electrocautery.     Specimen was confirmed margin by using intra-operative U/S, seen mass within specimen and free all margins.    The breast tissue was re-approximated using vicryl 3-0.    Skin edges were approximated with Vicryl 3-0 deep dermal sutures, and the skin was closed with Monocryl 5-0 subcuticular sutures.     The wound was cleansed, dried, and dressed with sterile gauze.

column29: -The patient was in supine position and general anesthesia was induced.    Lt breast  -Preoperative US localization was performed by radiology.  -The patient was placed supine and after GA the left chest and axilla were prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion.  -A circumareolar incision was made overlying the marking that was planned in such a way as to minimize the amount of dissection to reach the mass.  -Flaps were raised and the dissection was then taken down circumferentially, taking care to include the entire localizing marking and a wide margin of grossly normal tissue.  -The specimen was removed and oriented for radiologic ultrasound confirmation  -Radiologist confirm that the entire target lesion had been completed resected.   -The wound was irrigated  -Hemostasis was checked  -The wound was closed with interrupted sutures of 3-0 Vicryl and a subcuticular suture of 4-0 Monocryl    Rt breast  -A incision was made that encompassed the nipple-areola complex and the previous surgical scar and passed in a generally transverse direction across the breast  -Flaps were raised in the avascular plane between subcutaneous tissue and breast tissue from the clavicle superiorly, the sternum medially, the anterior rectus sheath inferiorly, and past the lateral border of the pectoralis major muscle laterally.   -Hemostasis was achieved in the flaps.   -The breast tissue and underlying pectoralis fascia were excised from the pectoralis major muscle, progressing from medially to laterally.   -At the lateral border of the pectoralis major muscle, the breast tissue was swung laterally and a lateral pedicle identified where breast tissue gave way to fat of axilla.  -The lateral pedicle was incised, ligated and the specimen removed.  -The wound was irrigated and hemostasis was achieved.   -A radivac suction drain was/ closed suction drains were brought into the operative field through a separate stab incision.  -The wound was closed with interrupted 3-0 Vicryl to the subcutaneous layer, followed by a subcuticular layer of 4-0 Vicryl.

column29: Bilateral breast mass excision under U/S mark  Left  - a curvilinear incision was made at L2 as marked by U/S.  - Subcutaneous tissue and breast tissue was dissected electrocautery, making sure to include all margin as guided by ultrasonography.  - Specimen was removed then sutured to mark sides of the margin; long single silk = lateral, long double silk = medial, short single silk=superior, short double silk=medial, short silk and long silk on different ends=anterior.  ---> specimen was confirmed to be margin free by the radiologist.   - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Subcutaneous layer was sutured by Vicryl 3-0.  - Dermal layer was sutured by Monocryl 4-0 in a continuous fashion.    Right  - a curvilinear incision was made at R10 as marked by U/S.  - Subcutaneous tissue and breast tissue was dissected electrocautery, making sure to include all margin as guided by ultrasonography.  - Specimen was removed then sutured to mark sides of the margin; long single silk = lateral, long double silk = medial, short single silk=superior, short double silk=medial, short silk and long silk on different ends=anterior.  ---> specimen was confirmed to be margin free by the radiologist.   - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Subcutaneous layer was sutured by Vicryl 3-0.  - Dermal layer was sutured by Monocryl 4-0 in a continuous fashion.    Rt ALND  - A curvilinear incision was made at around the lateral border of Rt pectoralis major muscle (as the patient old surgical scar)  - Subcutaneous tissue was dissected by electrocautery.  - Clavipectoral fascia was entered to explore the axillary content.  - Dissection was done until axillary vein was identified superiorly and LD muscle identified laterally.  - Bulky lymph nodes were identified.  - Some of intercostobrachial nerve were ligated then divided along with those bulky nodes.  - Otherwise, long thoracic nerve, thoracodorsal bundle, and some of intercostobrachial nerve branches were identified and preserved.  - Lymph nodes bulk was removed.  - Lymphatic vessels and small veins were ligated by metallic clip and silk 3-0.  - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Radivac drain was inserted x 1 at Rt axilla.  - Subcutaneous layer was sutured by Vicryl 3-0.  - Dermal layer was sutured by Monocryl 4-0 in a continuous fashion.

column28: - Enlarged left thyroid lobe  - Enlarged central nodes + neck node gr. II, IV, V   - No gross extrathyroidal extension   - Recurrent laryngeal nerves identified and preserved bilaterally   - Parathyroid glands identified and preserved with intact vascular supply
column29: - The patient was placed in supine position and underwent generalized anesthesia.  - Then the patient was placed in neck extension and heads up.  - A hockey stick incision was made approximately 2 FBs above the sternal notch.  - Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the sternal notch.  - The strap muscles were divided in the midline and retracted laterally to expose the thyroid glands.  - The delphian node was identified and dissected.  - The right thyroid lobe was mobilized first. - The right middle thyroid vein was identified, ligated and divided using harmonic scalpel.  - The right recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified in the tracheo-esophageal groove and carefully preserved.   - The right superior pole vessels were individually ligated and divided close to the thyroid capsule, preserving the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve using harmonic scalpel.  - The right superior and inferior parathyroid glands were identified and preserved with their vascular supply intact.   - The right lobe was dissected off the trachea and the isthmus was divided and removed along with the right thyroid lobe.  - The pre-tracheal node was identifed and dissected.  - Attention was then turned to the left side, The left thyroid lobe was mobilized in a similar fashion.  - The left middle thyroid vein was ligated and divided. The left superior pole vessels were ligated close to the thyroid capsule  - The left recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and preserved. The left superior and inferior parathyroid glands were identified and preserved. The left thyroid lobe was dissected free from trachea and removed, completing the total thyroidectomy.  - Then the central node (neck node gr.VI) was identified and dissected.  - The Sternocleidomastoid muscle was indentified and preserved.  - The neck node group II, IV was identified and dissected.  - The omohyoid muscle was identified and cut to access the deep structures of the neck.  - The internal jugular vein was identified and preserved.  - The neck nodes group V was identified and dissected.  - The spinal accessory nerve was identified at Erb's point and carefully preserved.  - Hemostasis was meticulously achieved.   - Radivac drain no.8 was placed at lateral neck and fixed by using silk 3-0.  - Surgicel was placed at both sides of trachea.  - Strap muscle was closed using Catgut 3-0 in continuous fashion.  - Platysma was closed using vicryl 4-0 in interrupted suture.  - Skin was closed using vicryl 4-0 in subcuticular suture.

column29: - Patient was in supine position with neck hyperextension supported by towel rolls underneath interscapular region and silicone gel under occipital region. Skin was prepped and draped in sterile technique.  - Low collar incision was done.  - Subplatysmal flaps were created. Upper flap was mobilized cranially up to thyroid cartilage. Lower flap was mobilized caudally up to suprasternal notch.  - Left anterior jugular vein was ligated with Silk 2-0. Right anterior jugular vein was preserved.  - Strap muscles were separated in the middle and retracted laterally.  - Pre-tracheal fascia was incised in midline  - Dissection laterally in between pre-tracheal fascia and capsule of thyroid gland was done.  - Some part of strap muscle was excised due to adhesion with thyroid gland.  - Lateral part of a left lobe was mobilized using Harmonic scarpel. Left middle thyroid vein was ligated with Silk 3-0.  - Left superior thyroid pedicle and left superior thyroid artery was clamped and ligated with Silk 3-0.  - Left inferior thyroid pedicle was exposed, clamped and divided.  - Lateral part of left thyroid gland was retracted medially. Left inferior thyroid artery was exposed.  - Left inferior thyroid artery branches were ligated with Silk 3-0, at the surface of the gland.   - Left superior parathyroid gland was identified and left in place.  - Berry's ligament was divided. Left recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and preserved. Thyroid gland was mobilized medially.  - The same procedure was done with right thyroid gland. Right recurrent laryngeal nerve was also preserved.  - Thyroid was dissected from anterior trachea.  - Wound was cleaned with NSS.  - Bleeding was checked and stopped.  - Surgicel was packed at both sides of trachea.  - Radivac drain was placed xI.  - Strap muscle was approximated with Chromic catgut 3-0, continuous suture.  - Platysma muscle was closed with Vicryl 4-0.  - Skin was closed with subcuticular stitch, Monocryl 4-0.

column28: - Two right breast lesions were identified preoperatively at R3 and R9 positions by ultrasound marking.  - Both lesions were localized successfully and excised through a single incision.  - The excised specimens contained the targeted lesions, comfirmed by US
column29: - The patient was placed in the supine position. General anesthesia was induced and prophylactic antibiotics were administered. The right breast was prepared and draped in a sterile fashion.  - Preoperative ultrasound-guided skin marking was checked to localize the lesions at R3 and R9 positions.   - A single curvilinear incision was made over the marked area to allow access to both lesions. Dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue to the breast parenchyma.  - The lesion at R3 was identified and excised with an adequate margin of surrounding breast tissue. Dissection was continued through the same incision toward the R9 lesion, which was similarly identified and excised. Both specimens were oriented and sent for pathological examination.  - The operative field was inspected, and meticulous hemostasis was achieved. The wound was irrigated and closed in layers using absorbable sutures for the deep tissue and subcuticular sutures for the skin. Sterile dressing was applied.
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column28: - The left breast was removed en bloc with overlying skin and nipple-areolar complex.  - No gross invasion of tumor to the pectoralis major muscle.  - No obvious chest wall involvement.  - Sentinel lymph node were identified using patent V blue and were successfully identified and excised. (Frozen section could not be performed due to frozen-section machine malfunction)  - No grossly enlarged or suspicious axillary lymph nodes apart from sentinel nodes.  - Hemostasis was secured with no active bleeding.  - Surgical margins appeared grossly free of tumor.
column29: - General anesthesia was induced, and prophylactic antibiotics were administered. A peri-areolar injection of patent V blue was performed. The left breast and axilla were prepared and draped in a sterile fashion.  - An elliptical incision was made incorporating the nipple-areolar complex.  dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue. Using visual identification.  - the sentinel lymph nodes were identified and excised. The lymphatic channels were ligated. Hemostasis was achieved.  - Skin flaps were raised superiorly to the clavicle, medially to the sternum, inferiorly to the inframammary fold, and laterally to the latissimus dorsi. The breast tissue was dissected off the pectoralis major fascia and removed en bloc.  - The operative field was inspected, and meticulous hemostasis was achieved. A closed-suction drain was placed in the mastectomy bed. The wound was closed in layers using absorbable sutures. Sterile dressing was applied.
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column29: -A  8-cm incision was made in a skin crease positioned approximately two finger breadths superior to the sternal notch.   -The subcutaneous tissues and platysma were divided   -Subplatysmal flaps were then raised inferiorly extending to the sternal notch and superiorly extending to the thyroid cartilage.   -The strap muscles were divided in the midline and retracted laterally.  -The left thyroid lobe was mobilized from its areolar attachments using blunt dissection, electrocautery and Hamonic energy device.   -The thyroid was gently retracted medially and the middle thyroid vein was identified and ligated with 3-0 silk   -The left thyroid gland was then gently retracted inferomedially.   -The superior pole vessels were ligated with 2-0 silk sutures and divided close to the thyroid gland, with careful attention not to injure the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve.  -The thyroid gland was gently retracted medially and the muscles retracted laterally using blunt dissection  -The Lt. inferior thyroid vessels were ligated with 3-0 silk ties and divided with great care to avoid injury to the recurrent laryngeal nerve and to preserve their distal branches.   -The  Lt. inferior parathyroid glands was identified and carefully preserved  -The thyroid was dissected medially from its attachments to the trachea up to the ligament of Berry  -Dissection was extended to the isthmus and it was dissected free from its attachments over the thyroid cartilage and then reflected inferiorly down to the isthmus.  -A similar procedure was then carried out on the right.   -The right recurrent laryngeal nerve was identified and carefully followed superficially along its course superiorly to ensure it was not injured.   -The right superior parathyroid glands was identified and preserved.   -Dissection was extended to the isthmus, dissecting the thyroid free from the trachea.   -The specimen was removed  -The wound was copiously irrigated and hemostasis was achieved  -Radivac drain was placed in surgical site   -Pre-tracheal fascia was reapproximated with a running 3-0 Vicryl sutures  -The skin was closed with a running 4-0 Monocryl subcuticular suture.

column28: •	Bilateral thyroid gland enlargement   •	Highly vascular gland with friable parenchyma   •	RLNs identified and preserved bilaterally  •	One parathyroid gland (right superior) devascularized → autoimplanted into right SCM  	•	No lymphadenopathy or invasion
column29: The patient was placed in a supine position with the neck extended using a shoulder roll. After induction of general anesthesia and endotracheal intubation, the neck and upper chest were prepped and draped in a sterile fashion.    A 6 cm low transverse cervical collar incision was made approximately 2 cm above the sternal notch. Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the thyroid cartilage and inferiorly to the sternal notch. Strap muscles were separated in the midline and retracted laterally.    The thyroid gland was markedly enlarged and highly vascular bilaterally, consistent with Graves' disease. There was no evidence of invasive disease or suspicious lymphadenopathy.    Dissection was performed using a harmonic scalpel to minimize thermal spread and reduce blood loss. The middle thyroid veins were identified and divided. The superior thyroid vessels were divided close to the thyroid capsule to preserve the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve. The recurrent laryngeal nerves (RLNs) were identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.    The parathyroid glands were identified. The left superior and right superior parathyroids were preserved in situ with intact vascular pedicles. The right superior parathyroid gland was noted to be devascularized during dissection.    The devascularized parathyroid was excised and placed in iced saline. It was minced into 1 mm³ fragments. A small pocket was created in the right sternocleidomastoid muscle using electrocautery. The minced parathyroid tissue was implanted into the muscle pocket and secured with a traumatic silk 3-0 sutures x2 to prevent displacement.    Hemostasis was confirmed. No significant bleeding noted. The strap muscles and platysma were reapproximated with absorbable sutures. Skin was closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl.    The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.
column37: The right superior parathyroid gland was noted to be devascularized during dissection.
column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column29: -The patient was placed in the supine position. The neck was extended with a shoulder roll. -The neck was prepped and draped in the standard sterile fashion.  -An elliptical incision was made to include edge of thyroid laterally both side. Subplatysmal flaps were raised superiorly to the notch of the thyroid cartilage and mandible, and inferiorly to the clavicles.  -The strap muscles were separated in the midline and tract laterally both side.  - Left Lobe: The left thyroid lobe was mobilized. The middle thyroid vein was ligated. The superior pole vessels were individually ligated close to the capsule to preserve the External Branch of the Superior Laryngeal Nerve. The left RLN was identified in the tracheoesophageal groove. The nerve was traced to its insertion at the cricothyroid joint. The parathyroid glands were identified and carefully dissected to preserve their vascular pedicle. The lobe was dissected off the trachea.  - Right Lobe: The right lobe was addressed similarly. However, there's an extrathyroidal extension to Rt strap muscle. The Right RLN was identified. Parathyroid glands were preserved.    - The sternocleidomastoid (SCM) muscle was retracted to expose the carotid sheath.  - The dissection was carried out within the boundaries of the clavicle inferiorly, the carotid artery medially, and the anterior border of the trapezius laterally and superiorly upto cervical LN level Ia and Ib  -Levels II, III, IV: The packet of nodes was dissected off the internal jugular vein (IJV) and carotid artery. The cervical plexus sensory branches were sacrificed as needed for clearance, but the phrenic nerve and brachial plexus were protected.  - The surgical field was irrigated with sterile saline. No evidence of chyle leak or active bleeding was noted.  - 2 radivac drains were placed in the right and left thyroid bed and lateral neck gutter and secured.  - The strap muscles were re-approximated with 3-0 absorbable suture. The platysma was closed with interrupted absorbable sutures. The skin was closed with vicryl 4/0 at dermis and subcuticular   The patiend was ectubated without tracheomalacia

Dissection was performed using a harmonic scalpel to minimize thermal spread and reduce blood loss. The superior thyroid vessels were divided close to the thyroid capsule to preserve the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve. The recurrent laryngeal nerves (RLNs) were identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.    Lt thyroid was further performed    The middle thyroid veins were identified and divided. The superior thyroid vessels were divided close to the thyroid capsule to preserve the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve. The recurrent laryngeal nerves (RLNs) were identified and carefully preserved bilaterally throughout their course in the tracheoesophageal groove.    The parathyroid glands were identified. The right and left superior and right superior parathyroids were preserved in situ with intact vascular pedicles.         Hemostasis was confirmed. No significant bleeding noted. The strap muscles and platysma were reapproximated with absorbable sutures. Skin was closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl. column29: A curvilinear incision was made over the lesion along the Langer's line. Dissection was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue using electrocautery. The breast tissue was dissected circumferentially , The lesion was excised en bloc along with surrounding breast tissue, ensuring adequate margin by U/S conformation.    The specimen was excised en bloc and oriented with sutures for margin identification. Hemostasis was achieved with electrocautery.     Bleeding was checked and stopped.    Skin edges were approximated with Vicryl 3-0 deep dermal sutures, and the skin was closed with Monocryl 4-0 subcuticular sutures. The wound was cleansed, dried, and dressed with sterile gauze

column38: Stable: Successful surgery and are stable, with normal vital signs

column29: - the patient was placed in supine position and underwent generalized anesthesia  - then the right arm was abducted 90 degrees  - Patent V blue dye 3 ml was injected in periareolar and peritumoral region  - the skin of right chest wall and right upper arm (include axilla) were cleaned and draped with antiseptic solution  - Transverse incison was done at the lowest crease of the axilla to do SLNB  - The stained LN was removed and send for frozen section: 3 SLNs were retrieved and the results was 0/3  - An elliptical incision encompassing the NAC and the mass.
